verdict.answer_first: B states the likely cause immediately: "Most likely: your access tokens have a short expiry...refresh path isn't actually working"; A opens with a category split: "one of two distinct failure shapes."
verdict.decisiveness: B commits harder to one main diagnosis with "Most likely"; A frames the answer as "one of two" and ends asking for more narrowing.
verdict.fabrication: A overclaims with "This is the #1 cause" without evidence; B also says "almost always," but less numerically absolute.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it gives verification-error checks: "Log the JWT verification error reason" and "echo $JWT_SECRET...on every instance."
